title: Penstemon tusharensis
intro: Penstemon tusharensis, commonly known as Tushar beardtongue, is a perennial flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Penstemon tusharensis include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote respiratory health and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
